title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
What Are Some Investment Options for Women?
As more and more women step out of the regular expectation cycle of marrying young, staying at home to raise kids, and managing the household, the importance of being financially independent is increasing as well. However, to be financially independent, the first prerequisite is to be financially literate. Only when you fully understand what you are putting your money into would you be able to get over the financial anxieties and make well-informed and independent investment decisions. Thankfully today there are numerous investment products in the market that are considered to be the  investment options for women. Knowing where and how to invest can make a big difference to your financial well-being.
Listed below are the 10  investment options for women:
Mutual Funds:
Mutual fund investments are relatively lower in risks in comparison to other higher-risk investment models. There are two ways in which one can invest in mutual funds, one is through a lump sum, and the other is through SIPs (Systematic Investment Plans). Under SIP, one can make a regular investment for months. It diversifies the risk appetite of a person and can work as a medium-risk investment option. Other than SIPs, an individual may choose to invest a lump amount and leave it for a longer tenure to get higher returns.
Gold
For Indian women, investing in gold has always been a golden move. Being a luxury good, the price of gold is likely to always grow. Considered to be an inflation-proof luxury good, gold can be an ideal investment for women. You can go for raw gold, gold coins or biscuits, jewellery, or even try online paper gold investment schemes.
Public Provident Fund1
When discussing an investment plan with no risk and tax benefits, PPF (Public Provident Fund) is an excellent option. With a lock-in period of 15 years (that can be extended to 20 years), a PPF account offers more than 7% returns. All those women who wish to opt for a no-risk investment option can go for a PPF account. It requires a minimum of INR 500 investment per year. However, you may choose to invest up to INR 1,50,000/per year as well.
Employees Provident Fund (EPF)2
EPF allows an individual to make risk-free investments with decent returns. As per the new rules, newly employed working women need to contribute only 8% of their salary for the first 3 years. Apart from being risk-free, EPF also provides tax exemption up to INR 1.5 lakh for investment under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act.
Kisan Vikas Patra3
The post office of India offers a high returns short-term investment plan. Under Kisan Vikas Patra, a woman can invest for 9 years and get double the investment amount as returns post-maturity. The minimum investment is INR 1,000/year with no cap on the maximum limit. However, the investment must be in the multiple of INR 1,000.
Unit Linked Insurance Plans1
A one-stop solution for both investment and life cover is a Unit Linked Insurance Plan. The premium paid towards ULIPs are divided into two parts; one goes towards life insurance and the other part is invested in equities for regular income post-maturity. You can also get tax benefits up to INR 1.5 lakhs on the premium that is paid.
Post Office Time Deposit Schemes
When it comes to choosing the right investment options for women, especially those who have specific financial goals for their children, can choose to invest in a post office time deposit. The tenure ranges from 1 to 5 years and yields decent returns upon maturity. However, no premature withdrawal is allowed.
Post Office Monthly Income Scheme4
Upon retirement, women may look for risk-free and short-term plans that offer stable returns. One such plan is the monthly income or fixed deposit scheme of the post office. This scheme is offered by the Government of India and offers 7.6% returns/annum. The tenure is of 5 years with a minimum investment of INR 1,500/ year and a maximum investment of INR 4,50,000/year for a single account and INR 9,00,000/annum for a joint account.
Fixed Deposits
Investment in fixed deposits has been one of the investment options, and it seems that it can never lose its significance. This is because of its stable and high returns upon maturity. A fixed deposit is a fantastic arrangement if you are a woman looking forward to a low to no-risk investment plan. Here, you need to fix a sum of money for a fixed number of years. Some institutions may offer as high as 7% returns/annum. However, this rate can vary. You can use an investment calculator to better understand the returns you may earn.
National Saving Certificate
The post office of India offers NSC. With a high return of 8%/annum, this investment plan is a ideal option for women planning to invest and save for the future. This investment plan has no maximum investment limit. The investor cannot pull out any amount before maturity except for admissible causes like the death of the account holder.
Conclusion
The ability to make financial decisions is indeed a liberty that women should surely enjoy. Whether you are a homemaker or a working woman, it is important to be financially literate, as only then would you be able to make important and fruitful investment plans for yourself and your family.
